verses.commentary: New creation day. Still dark. Mary first. Stone removed. Empty tomb discovered.

So she came running to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we don't know where they have put Him! Running report. Peter and beloved disciple. Assumption: body stolen. Grave robbery feared. Resurrection not imagined.
Both go. Investigation begins.
John faster. Arrives first. Youthful speed.
Linen visible. Hesitation. Does not enter. Deference or fear.
Enters immediately. Bold. Observes grave clothes.
Soudarion (σουδάριον), face cloth. Order preserved. Not hastily unwrapped. Body passed through.
Sees and believes. First believer in resurrection. Grave clothes convince. Empty tomb plus orderly burial clothes equal resurrection.
They still did not understand from Scripture that Jesus had to rise from the dead. Scripture understanding lacking. Resurrection prophesied but not grasped. Sight precedes scriptural comprehension.
Processing. No announcement yet. Private discovery.
Mary remains. Grief. Weeping. Looks inside. Persistent seeking.
Two angels. White garments. Positioned where body lay. Head and foot. Ark of covenant imagery (Exodus 25:18-22).
They asked her, Woman, why are you crying? Who is it you are looking for? Angels inquire. Reason for tears. Object of search. Gentle questioning.
Sees Jesus. Does not recognize. Tears blur. Unexpected appearance. Resurrection body different.

He asked her, Woman, why are you crying? Who is it you are looking for? Thinking He was the gardener, she said, Sir, if you have carried Him away, tell me where you have put Him, and I will get Him. Jesus repeats angels' question. Mistaken for gardener. Ironic. New Adam in garden. Requests body location. Devoted love.

Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned toward Him and cried out in Aramaic, Rabboni! (which means Teacher). Personal address. Name spoken. Immediate recognition. Voice reveals identity. Rabboni: my teacher. Intimate title. John 10:3, sheep know shepherd's voice.

Jesus said, Do not hold on to Me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. Go instead to My brothers and tell them, I am ascending to My Father and your Father, to My God and your God. Not yet ascended. Relationship changing. Commission: go tell. Brothers: new status for disciples. My Father and your Father. Shared sonship. My God and your God. Shared worship.

Mary Magdalene went to the disciples with the news: I have seen the Lord! And she told them that He had said these things to her. First evangelist. Apostle to apostles. I have seen. Heōraka (ἑώρακα). Perfect tense. Permanent sight. Message delivered.

On the evening of that first day of the week, when the disciples were together, with the doors locked for fear of the Jewish leaders, Jesus came and stood among them and said, Peace be with you! Sunday evening. Locked doors. Fear. Jesus appears. Passes through barriers. Peace greeting. Shalom.
Nail prints. Spear wound. Proof of identity. Joy replaces fear. Sight produces joy.
Again Jesus said, Peace be with you! As the Father has sent Me, I am sending you. Peace repeated. Commission given. As Father sent Son, Son sends disciples. Apostolic mission parallels incarnation.

And with that He breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit. Enephysēsen (ἐνεφύσησεν), breathed into. Genesis 2:7 echo. New creation. Receive Holy Spirit. Anticipation of Pentecost or immediate empowerment. Spirit imparted.

If you forgive anyone's sins, their sins are forgiven; if you do not forgive them, they are not forgiven. Authority to forgive. Apostolic power. Declarative authority. Binding and loosing. Gospel proclamation brings forgiveness or retention based on belief.
Missed first appearance. Unfortunate timing.

So the other disciples told him, We have seen the Lord! But he said to them, Unless I see the nail marks in His hands and put my finger where the nails were, and put my hand into His side, I will not believe. Testimony given. Thomas demands proof. Empirical requirement. Touch, not just sight. Honest doubt.

A week later His disciples were in the house again, and Thomas was with them. Though the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said, Peace be with you! Eight days later. Thomas present. Doors locked again. Jesus appears. Same greeting.

Then He said to Thomas, Put your finger here; see My hands. Reach out your hand and put it into My side. Stop doubting and believe. Jesus offers exact proof Thomas demanded. Omniscience. Knew Thomas' words. Invites touch. Command to believe.

Thomas said to Him, My Lord and my God! Highest confession in John. Ho kyrios mou kai ho theos mou (ὁ κύριός μου καὶ ὁ θεός μου). Lord and God. Full deity affirmed. No rebuke from Jesus. Acceptance equals validation.

Then Jesus told him, Because you have seen Me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed. Sight-based faith acknowledged. But greater blessing for non-seeing faith. Future believers in view. Faith without sight is higher faith.
Many signs. Not all recorded. Limited scope acknowledged.
Why John wrote. Evangelistic intent. Believe Jesus is Messiah. Believe Jesus is Son of God. Result: life in His name. Pisteuō (πιστεύω), believe, trust. Eternal life through faith.
